sections.overview: A scheme by the Ministry of Women & Child Development to support self-development and empowerment of out-of-school adolescent girls and improve their health, nutrition, and hygiene status. It is implemented through Anganwadi Centres under the ICDS platform.
sections.objective: To support out-of-school adolescent girls in transitioning to education or skill training, improve life skills and health awareness, and provide access to public services.
eligibility: Age must be between 11 and 14 years
benefits: Take-home ration or hot cooked meal providing 600 calories and 18–20 grams protein for 300 days
Financial support of ₹9.5 per beneficiary per day for nutrition
Counseling on family welfare and childcare practices
Life skill education and access to public services
application_process: Identification is done through a baseline survey conducted by Anganwadi Workers
Anganwadi Workers visit households to identify eligible girls
Benefits are provided through the local Anganwadi Centre
documents_required: Proof of Age or Date of Birth

faq.question: What is IFA Supplementation?
faq.answer: Iron and Folic Acid supplements are provided to address iron deficiency anaemia under the National Nutrition Anaemia Control Programme.

faq.question: When is the health check-up organized?
faq.answer: Health check-ups are organized at least once every three months during Kishori Diwas, often aligned with Village Health & Nutrition Day.

faq.question: What is Kishore Health Card?
faq.answer: It records weight, height, BMI, and other services provided and is maintained at Anganwadi Centres.

faq.question: Is a 12-year-old girl eligible?
faq.answer: Yes, if she meets the criteria, she is eligible.

faq.question: Can multiple girls from the same family apply?
faq.answer: Yes, multiple eligible girls from the same family can apply.

faq.question: Is there any application fee?
faq.answer: No, the application process is free.

faq.question: Do I need to visit an AWC for identification?
faq.answer: No, the Anganwadi Worker will visit households to collect details.

faq.question: Will Aadhaar details be public?
faq.answer: No, Aadhaar information is collected with consent and kept secure as per guidelines.

faq.question: Do I need to submit original documents?
How is the survey data verified?
faq.answer: Supervisors verify around 20% of entries for authenticity.

faq.question: Where can I find the survey form?
faq.answer: It is available in the annexure of the scheme guidelines.
